content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>添加完成</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-04-21</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on durable technical signal:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)
- Concrete debugging or investigative findings from logs, traces, queue state, database rows, and code-path inspection

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored, discovered, confirmed, traced

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built or learned):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"
- "Observation queue for claude-mem session timed out waiting for an agent pool slot"
- "Fallback processing abandoned pending messages after Gemini and OpenRouter returned 404"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings with no follow-on finding
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- File related research that comes back empty or not found

If skipping, return an empty response only. Do not explain the skip in prose.

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

  <what_happened>WebFetch</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-04-21T03:40:53.314Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>/mnt/d/assistant</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"url\":\"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ikook-wang/cc-sync/main/install.sh\",\"prompt\":\"Show me the full content of this install script - I need to see what files it creates (sync.sh, sync.conf, etc) and their contents\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"bytes\":10071,\"code\":200,\"codeText\":\"OK\",\"result\":\"The install script you shared downloads most files from the repository rather than embedding them inline. Here's what it creates and uses:\\n\\n**Files Downloaded from Repo (not shown in install script):**\\n\\n- `sync.sh` - Downloaded from `$REPO_URL/sync.sh`\\n- `uninstall.sh` - Downloaded from `$REPO_URL/uninstall.sh`\\n- `.gitignore` (via `gitignore.template`) - Downloaded from `$REPO_URL/gitignore.template`\\n\\n**Files Created by the Script:**\\n\\n`sync.conf` - Created with this content:\\n```\\n# cc-sync configuration\\n# https://github.com/ikook-wang/cc-sync\\nKEEP_SESSIONS=<user input, default 3>\\n```\\n\\n`settings.json` - Patched via Python to add a `SessionStart` hook with a git pull command.\\n\\n**Shell Wrapper** - Added to `~/.zshrc` or `~/.bashrc`:\\n```bash\\nclaude() {\\n  command claude \\\"$@\\\"\\n  bash ~/.claude/sync.sh &>/dev/null &\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Summary:** The install script itself doesn't contain the full content of `sync.sh` or `uninstall.sh`—those are fetched at runtime from the GitHub repository. You'd need to view the raw files at:\\n- `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ikook-wang/cc-sync/main/sync.sh`\\n- `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ikook-wang/cc-sync/main/uninstall.sh`\\n- `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ikook-wang/cc-sync/main/gitignore.template`\\n\\nto see their complete contents.\",\"durationMs\":8997,\"url\":\"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ikook-wang/cc-sync/main/install.sh\"}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

The remote URL was updated from a previous HTTPS URL to the SSH equivalent before pushing. The master branch is now tracked against the remote, enabling future syncing of Claude configuration across environments.</narrative>

```xml
  </concepts>
  <files_read/>
  <files_modified/>
</observation>
```

content.thinking: The user is investigating the cc-sync tool (https://github.com/ikook-wang/cc-sync) and downloading its sync.sh script. Let me record the discovery of cc-sync's architecture and the download of sync.sh.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>cc-sync tool architecture discovered</title>
  <subtitle>The cc-sync install script patches settings.json with a SessionStart hook and wraps the claude CLI command.</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>cc-sync repo: https://github.com/ikook-wang/cc-sync</fact>
    <fact>install.sh creates sync.conf with KEEP_SESSIONS setting (default 3) at ~/.claude/sync.conf</fact>
    <fact>install.sh patches ~/.claude/settings.json via Python to add a SessionStart hook that runs git pull</fact>
    <fact>install.sh adds a shell wrapper in ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc that runs sync.sh in background after each claude invocation</fact>
    <fact>sync.sh, uninstall.sh, and gitignore.template are downloaded from the repo at install time, not embedded in install.sh</fact>
    <fact>sync.sh downloaded from raw.githubusercontent.com/ikook-wang/cc-sync/main/sync.sh and saved to ~/.claude/sync.sh with execute permission</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The cc-sync project automates syncing of the ~/.claude directory to GitHub. Its install script does three things: creates a sync.conf config file, patches settings.json to add a git pull on SessionStart, and wraps the claude CLI so sync.sh runs in the background after each session. The actual sync logic lives in sync.sh which was fetched separately. This was being researched and partially adopted — sync.sh was downloaded to ~/.claude/sync.sh.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read/>
  <files_modified>
    <file>~/.claude/sync.sh</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

content:
<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Read</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-04-21T03:43:44.263Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>/mnt/d/assistant</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"/home/nan/.claude/sync.sh\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"/home/nan/.claude/sync.sh\",\"content\":\"#!/usr/bin/env bash\\n# cc-sync: Claude Code configuration sync script\\n# https://github.com/ikook-wang/cc-sync\\n\\nCLAUDE_DIR=\\\"$HOME/.claude\\\"\\nCONF_FILE=\\\"$CLAUDE_DIR/sync.conf\\\"\\nVERBOSE=false\\n\\n# Parse arguments\\nfor arg in \\\"$@\\\"; do\\n  case \\\"$arg\\\" in\\n    --verbose|-v) VERBOSE=true ;;\\n  esac\\ndone\\n\\nlog() { $VERBOSE && echo \\\"[cc-sync] $*\\\"; }\\n\\n# Read config\\nKEEP_SESSIONS=3\\nif [ -f \\\"$CONF_FILE\\\" ]; then\\n  # shellcheck source=/dev/null\\n  . \\\"$CONF_FILE\\\"\\nfi\\n\\ncd \\\"$CLAUDE_DIR\\\" || exit 0\\n\\n# Ensure we're in a git repo with a remote\\ngit rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree &>/dev/null || exit 0\\ngit remote get-url origin &>/dev/null || exit 0\\n\\nlog \\\"Pulling remote changes...\\\"\\ngit pull --no-rebase --autostash --quiet 2>/dev/null || {\\n  log \\\"Merge conflict detected, auto-resolving with local version...\\\"\\n  git checkout --ours . 2>/dev/null\\n  git add -A 2>/dev/null\\n  git commit -m \\\"sync: auto-resolve merge conflicts\\\" --quiet 2>/dev/null || true\\n}\\n\\nlog \\\"Processing session files (keeping $KEEP_SESSIONS per project)...\\\"\\n\\n# Iterate over each project directory\\nfor project_dir in projects/*/; do\\n  [ -d \\\"$project_dir\\\" ] || continue\\n\\n  # Find .jsonl files sorted by modification time (newest first)\\n  # Compatible with bash 3.2 (no mapfile)\\n  jsonl_files=()\\n  while IFS= read -r file; do\\n    [ -n \\\"$file\\\" ] && jsonl_files+=(\\\"$file\\\")\\n  done < <(find \\\"$project_dir\\\" -maxdepth 1 -name \\\"*.jsonl\\\" -exec ls -t {} + 2>/dev/null)\\n\\n  [ ${#jsonl_files[@]} -eq 0 ] && continue\\n\\n  log \\\"  $project_dir: ${#jsonl_files[@]} sessions found\\\"\\n\\n  # Force-add the most recent N sessions\\n  for ((i=0; i<KEEP_SESSIONS && i<${#jsonl_files[@]}; i++)); do\\n    git add -f \\\"${jsonl_files[$i]}\\\" 2>/dev/null || true\\n    log \\\"    + ${jsonl_files[$i]}\\\"\\n\\n    # Also add the corresponding UUID directory (subagents, tool-results)\\n    uuid_dir=\\\"${jsonl_files[$i]%.jsonl}\\\"\\n    if [ -d \\\"$uuid_dir\\\" ]; then\\n      git add -f \\\"$uuid_dir\\\" 2>/dev/null || true\\n    fi\\n  done\\n\\n  # Remove older sessions from git index only (keep local files)\\n  for ((i=KEEP_SESSIONS; i<${#jsonl_files[@]}; i++)); do\\n    if git ls-files --error-unmatch \\\"${jsonl_files[$i]}\\\" &>/dev/null; then\\n      git rm --cached --quiet \\\"${jsonl_files[$i]}\\\" 2>/dev/null || true\\n      log \\\"    - ${jsonl_files[$i]}\\\"\\n    fi\\n\\n    uuid_dir=\\\"${jsonl_files[$i]%.jsonl}\\\"\\n    if [ -d \\\"$uuid_dir\\\" ] && git ls-files --error-unmatch \\\"$uuid_dir\\\" &>/dev/null; then\\n      git rm -r --cached --quiet \\\"$uuid_dir\\\" 2>/dev/null || true\\n    fi\\n  done\\ndone\\n\\n# Stage all other tracked files\\ngit add -A 2>/dev/null || true\\n\\n# Commit and push if there are changes\\nif ! git diff --cached --quiet 2>/dev/null; then\\n  log \\\"Committing changes...\\\"\\n  git commit -m \\\"sync\\\" --quiet 2>/dev/null || true\\nfi\\n\\nlog \\\"Pushing to remote...\\\"\\ngit push --quiet 2>/dev/null || true\\n\\nlog \\\"Done.\\\"\\n\",\"numLines\":95,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":95}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

content.thinking: This is a detailed read of the sync.sh content - worth recording the key behavioral details of how it works.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>cc-sync sync.sh logic fully examined</title>
  <subtitle>sync.sh pulls remote changes, keeps only the N most recent JSONL session files per project, then commits and pushes.</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>sync.sh reads KEEP_SESSIONS from ~/.claude/sync.conf (defaults to 3 if not set)</fact>
    <fact>Performs git pull --no-rebase --autostash before any local changes; auto-resolves merge conflicts by checking out local version (--ours)</fact>
    <fact>Iterates over ~/.claude/projects/*/ directories, finds .jsonl files sorted newest-first</fact>
    <fact>Force-adds (git add -f) the KEEP_SESSIONS most recent .jsonl files plus their matching UUID subdirectories</fact>
    <fact>Removes older session files from the git index only (git rm --cached) — local files are preserved on disk</fact>
    <fact>Stages all other tracked files with git add -A, then commits with message "sync" and pushes</fact>
    <fact>Entire script exits silently if ~/.claude is not a git repo or has no remote configured</fact>
    <fact>Supports --verbose / -v flag for debug logging; silent by default</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The sync.sh script implements a selective session-aware git sync for ~/.claude. After pulling remote changes (with autostash and conflict auto-resolution favoring local), it manages session JSONL files by keeping only the newest KEEP_SESSIONS files per project in the git index, pruning older ones without deleting local copies. This prevents the repo from growing unbounded with session history while preserving all settings and recent context. The script is designed to run silently in the background after each claude invocation.</narrative>
```
